summary: Iter 28 — Bulletproof party autoplay + Party Joining Overlay verification. All 5 acceptance criteria PASS. [A] Visiting /title/movie/tt0816692?autoplay=1&party=ABC123&at_ms=0&position_ms=0 — within ~250ms the URL changed to /play?url=...&title=Interstellar&type=movie&imdbId=tt0816692&party=ABC123&at_ms=0&position_ms=0 (party qparam preserved through the redirect). The dedicated party-autoplay useEffect (Detail.jsx:298-320) fires regardless of 1080p label and the user's getAutoplay1080p() pref — exactly as designed. [B] [data-testid='party-joining-overlay'] appears immediately (within 250ms) with both the 'JOINING WATCH PARTY' header AND a status line that flips between 'Resolving stream…' (while streams load) and 'Starting playback in a moment…' (once streams are picked) — observed text 'JOINING WATCH PARTYStarting playback in a moment…'. [C] After navigate() fires (autoplayFiredRef=true), the overlay is removed from the DOM (verified by query_selector returning None on the /play page). [D] Non-party regression confirmed both ways: (D1) with the profile-scoped key `onnowtv-autoplay-1080p:tester-auto`='0' (pref OFF) and NO party= param, the page stays on /title/movie/... and the [data-testid='stream-picker'] is rendered with 2 EP-STREM streams visible — autoplay did NOT fire (correct). (D2) with the same key flipped to '1' (pref ON) and NO party= param, navigation goes to /play without any party= qparam — non-party autoplay still works via the autoplayCandidate (1080p) path. [E] Backend: 16/16 pytest tests in test_watch_party.py PASS (REST create/state, WS hello/pick/play/pause/chat/disconnect, ready handshake, guest join, reactions broadcast/whitelist/rate-limit). No regressions anywhere.
critical_code_review_comments: Detail.jsx:298-320 — new dedicated party-autoplay useEffect correctly gated only on (partyCode + autoplayRequested + type==='movie' + streams loaded + non-empty). No 1080p guard, no user-pref guard — exactly as the brief required. 5-tier fallback (1080p direct → any 1080p → first direct → first torrent → streams[0]) confirmed.

Detail.jsx:322-336 — old/regular autoplay useEffect now correctly bails when partyCode is set (line 327 `if (partyCode) return;`), preventing double-fire. The autoplayFiredRef guard further protects against double-fire.

Detail.jsx:520-562 — party-joining-overlay correctly conditional on `partyCode && !autoplayFiredRef.current`. Text flips between 'Resolving stream…' / 'Starting playback in a moment…' / 'No streams available' based on streamLoading / streams.length. pointerEvents:'none' is correct (overlay must not block underlying redirect).

MINOR: getAutoplay1080p() default is TRUE (prefs.js:20) — a user with default prefs and no party will already autoplay via the regular path. This matches the documented behaviour ('skips the source picker') but is worth surfacing in QA so testers explicitly call setAutoplay1080p(false) on a per-profile scoped key (`onnowtv-autoplay-1080p:<profileId>`) to verify the non-autoplay branch. No code change needed.

MINOR: CORS errors from https://torrentio.strem.fun show in console during Detail load — these are preview-pod-only (the addon list contains a torrentio entry whose CORS doesn't allow the preview origin). The Plex EP-STREM addon returns enough streams to make autoplay fire, so this is a no-op for tests. Production HK1 device makes the call from a native Android context and CORS doesn't apply.
test_credentials: Tester profile seeded in localStorage (onnowtv-profiles-v1 + onnowtv-active-profile-v1='tester-auto'); for the party flow vesper-party-role='guest' + vesper-party-member-id='test-member-1' in sessionStorage. For [D1] pref-OFF test, MUST set the profile-scoped key onnowtv-autoplay-1080p:tester-auto='0' (the legacy unscoped key alone is not enough — readScopedString suffixes the active profile id).
seed_data_creation: Ephemeral. Party code 'ABC123' is fake — backend never receives a hello frame because the test only checks the client-side autoplay-and-navigate path; the WS connection that the JS player would open afterwards fails ('WebSocket is closed before the connection is established') but that happens AFTER the autoplay assertion is already PASS.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Bulletproof party autoplay is fully fixed and verified. To retest in <30s: (1) seed onnowtv-profiles-v1 + onnowtv-active-profile-v1='tester-auto' + sessionStorage vesper-party-role/member-id; (2) goto /title/movie/<imdbId>?autoplay=1&party=<any-6-char-code>; (3) within ~1s the URL must contain '/play' AND '&party=<code>' — and [data-testid='party-joining-overlay'] must appear BEFORE the redirect, then be gone after. To test the non-party regression branch [D1], you MUST write the profile-scoped key `onnowtv-autoplay-1080p:tester-auto`='0' (NOT just the unscoped key — readScopedString in /app/frontend/src/lib/profileScope.js suffixes the active profile id). Backend 16/16 pytest tests in /app/backend/tests/test_watch_party.py is the regression suite — run with REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL=$(grep REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL /app/frontend/.env | cut -d= -f2) python -m pytest backend/tests/test_watch_party.py -v.
rca of the issue: Original user-reported regression: 'I clicked Play, my side showed Play 1080p button waiting for manual click, hers showed the stream picker — neither auto-fired.' RCA: the original autoplay useEffect (Detail.jsx:322-336) had TWO guards that could block party mode — (1) `if (!getAutoplay1080p()) return;` — the guest had Autoplay-1080p OFF in her profile so this returned early; and (2) `if (!autoplayCandidate) return;` — the host's stream list had no 1080p-labelled stream so candidate was null. Either guard alone was enough to leave the user on the Detail page. Fix: a new DEDICATED party-autoplay useEffect (lines 298-320) with NO 1080p guard and NO user-pref guard, plus a `if (partyCode) return;` bail in the old useEffect to prevent double-fire. A new [data-testid='party-joining-overlay'] visual reassures the user that something IS happening during the brief streams-resolving window. Verified end-to-end: party URL → overlay appears (~250ms) → URL navigates to /play with party= preserved (~250ms) → overlay removed from DOM. Bug fully closed.
